title: Mini Distillation Column Reflux Ratio Adaptive Controller
description: Monitors and controls a bench-scale ethanol-water distillation column (6 theoretical plates, 500 mL reboiler) using a six-point DS18B20 axial temperature profile. The Arduino computes the separation efficiency proxy from the reboiler-to-condenser temperature gradient and adapts the reflux ratio (R = 2–8) via timed solenoid valve switching. SSR controls reboiler heater power; all temperatures and valve states are logged to SD card. Fault trips on reboiler over-temperature or condenser temperature collapse.
objective: Maintain ethanol distillate purity proxy (condenser temperature ≤ 80 °C) across varying feed rates by adaptive reflux ratio adjustment, with full axial temperature profile logged at 0.2 Hz.
constraints: Arduino Uno, six DS18B20 probes, SSR reboiler heater, two solenoid valves, SD logger; max reboiler temperature 100 °C; max reboiler volume 500 mL; ethanol < 40 % v/v feed; ventilated enclosure required.
supporting_fact_titles: Distillation Column Mass and Energy Balance
design_element_titles: Multi-Point 1-Wire Temperature Array for Column Profiling

element_usage_notes.Multi-Point 1-Wire Temperature Array for: Six DS18B20 probes provide axial T profile; condenser temperature used as purity proxy; reboiler T for heater control.
element_usage_notes.Reflux Ratio Solenoid Splitter: Timed valve switching sets R; increased when condenser T rises above 80 °C setpoint.
element_usage_notes.SSR-Controlled Water Cooling Jacket: SSR repurposed as reboiler heater switch; bang-bang control maintains reboiler at 95 °C.
element_usage_notes.SD Card Data Logger Module: Logs all six temperatures, R, SSR state, and timestamp at each 5 s interval.

element_interactions.description: Rising condenser temperature triggers increase in reflux ratio to improve separation
